goal: Move backend-facing adapter/provider conversion and request semantics into Go while keeping TS runtime/core for hook, skill, tool, commander, and cowork execution.
deliverable: A frozen requirement document, an execution plan, and the first migration slices for Go-owned backend functionality.
constraints: Use team mode with leader-directed parallel reconnaissance
Reuse existing adapter and runtime methods instead of creating new abstractions
Do not move hook/skill execution into backend adapters
Do not create a second runtime or duplicate semantic truth source
Keep changes backward compatible unless explicitly scoped otherwise
acceptance_criteria: Requirement doc states exact Go-owned migration scope and explicit non-goals
Execution plan enumerates first migration waves with file-level ownership boundaries
Go truth anchors and TS runtime keep-boundaries are backed by file:line evidence
First migration slices are identified around backend-facing provider conversion and request semantics only
product_acceptance_criteria: Backend request semantics have one clear truth source in Go
TS runtime/core remains the only execution surface for hooks, skills, tools, commander, and cowork
No new parallel adapter abstraction is introduced
manual_spot_checks: Confirm Go adapter types already cover semantics and tool-turn request payloads
Confirm TS runtime still owns hook and skill execution entry points
Confirm migration plan excludes wholesale TS runtime relocation
completion_language_policy: Do not claim migration complete; only claim preparation artifacts and scoped migration plan are complete when evidenced.
delivery_truth_contract: Requirement and plan docs under docs/, plus receipts under outputs/runtime/vibe-sessions/, are the canonical truth for this preparation phase.
non_goals: Migrating HookManager, SkillDispatcher, HeadlessToolRuntime, Commander, or Cowork runtime into Go
Rebuilding provider behavior from scratch when an existing method already exists
Changing frontend/TUI behavior in this preparation phase
inferred_assumptions: Go backend should own backend-facing provider conversion and request semantics
TS runtime/core should remain execution-oriented rather than service/platform-oriented
The first implementation wave should favor deleting duplication over adding compatibility wrappers
